// ==== Cargo.toml ====
# <auto-generated/>
[package]
name = "koine-domain"
version = "0.0.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "Generated by Koine — do not edit by hand."

[dependencies]
rust_decimal = "1"
regex = "1"

[lib]
path = "src/lib.rs"

// ==== src/billing.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! The `Billing` bounded context.

use crate::koine_runtime::Decimal;

/// The errors a domain operation in this context can fail with.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum DomainError {
    /// An invariant of `type_name` was violated: `rule`.
    InvariantViolation { type_name: &'static str, rule: &'static str },
    /// Two quantities of different units were combined.
    UnitMismatch { type_name: &'static str },
}

impl std::fmt::Display for DomainError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name, rule } => write!(f, "{type_name}: {rule}"),
            DomainError::UnitMismatch { type_name } => write!(f, "{type_name}: units do not match"),
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for DomainError {}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Money {
    amount: Decimal,
    currency: Currency,
}

impl Money {
    /// Creates a validated `Money`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(amount: Decimal, currency: Currency) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        if !(amount >= Decimal::from(0i64)) {
            return Err(DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name: "Money", rule: "a monetary amount cannot be negative" });
        }
        Ok(Self {
            amount,
            currency,
        })
    }

    pub fn amount(&self) -> Decimal { self.amount }

    pub fn currency(&self) -> Currency { self.currency }
}

impl std::ops::Mul<i64> for Money {
    type Output = Money;
    fn mul(self, factor: i64) -> Money {
        Money::new(self.amount * Decimal::from(factor), self.currency)
            .expect("Money: `*` violated an invariant")
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum Currency {
    Eur,
    Usd,
    Gbp,
}

impl Currency {
    /// Looks up a variant by its declared name (a non-throwing `TryFromName`).
    pub fn from_name(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
        match name {
            "EUR" => Some(Currency::Eur),
            "USD" => Some(Currency::Usd),
            "GBP" => Some(Currency::Gbp),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Looks up a variant by its ordinal value (a non-throwing `TryFromValue`).
    pub fn from_value(value: i64) -> Option<Self> {
        match value {
            0 => Some(Currency::Eur),
            1 => Some(Currency::Usd),
            2 => Some(Currency::Gbp),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively maps the variant to a value — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn match_<R>(
        &self,
        eur: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        usd: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        gbp: impl FnOnce() -> R,
    ) -> R {
        match self {
            Currency::Eur => eur(),
            Currency::Usd => usd(),
            Currency::Gbp => gbp(),
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively dispatches a side effect — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn switch(
        &self,
        eur: impl FnOnce(),
        usd: impl FnOnce(),
        gbp: impl FnOnce(),
    ) {
        match self {
            Currency::Eur => eur(),
            Currency::Usd => usd(),
            Currency::Gbp => gbp(),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Email {
    raw: String,
}

impl Email {
    /// Creates a validated `Email`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(raw: String) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        if !(crate::koine_runtime::regex_is_match(r"^[^@]+@[^@]+$", &raw)) {
            return Err(DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name: "Email", rule: "invalid email address" });
        }
        Ok(Self {
            raw,
        })
    }

    pub fn raw(&self) -> &str { &self.raw }
}


/// A branded identity value.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct CustomerId(String);

impl CustomerId {
    pub fn new(value: impl Into<String>) -> Self { CustomerId(value.into()) }
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Customer {
    id: CustomerId,
    name: String,
    email: Email,
}

impl PartialEq for Customer {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool { self.id == other.id }
}
impl Eq for Customer {}
impl std::hash::Hash for Customer {
    fn hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) { self.id.hash(state); }
}

impl Customer {
    /// Creates a validated `Customer`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(id: CustomerId, name: String, email: Email) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        Ok(Self {
            id,
            name,
            email,
        })
    }

    pub fn id(&self) -> &CustomerId { &self.id }

    pub fn name(&self) -> &str { &self.name }

    pub fn email(&self) -> &Email { &self.email }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum OrderStatus {
    Draft,
    Placed,
    Shipped,
    Cancelled,
}

impl OrderStatus {
    /// Looks up a variant by its declared name (a non-throwing `TryFromName`).
    pub fn from_name(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
        match name {
            "Draft" => Some(OrderStatus::Draft),
            "Placed" => Some(OrderStatus::Placed),
            "Shipped" => Some(OrderStatus::Shipped),
            "Cancelled" => Some(OrderStatus::Cancelled),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Looks up a variant by its ordinal value (a non-throwing `TryFromValue`).
    pub fn from_value(value: i64) -> Option<Self> {
        match value {
            0 => Some(OrderStatus::Draft),
            1 => Some(OrderStatus::Placed),
            2 => Some(OrderStatus::Shipped),
            3 => Some(OrderStatus::Cancelled),
            _ => None,
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively maps the variant to a value — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn match_<R>(
        &self,
        draft: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        placed: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        shipped: impl FnOnce() -> R,
        cancelled: impl FnOnce() -> R,
    ) -> R {
        match self {
            OrderStatus::Draft => draft(),
            OrderStatus::Placed => placed(),
            OrderStatus::Shipped => shipped(),
            OrderStatus::Cancelled => cancelled(),
        }
    }

    /// Exhaustively dispatches a side effect — one arm per member, no catch-all.
    pub fn switch(
        &self,
        draft: impl FnOnce(),
        placed: impl FnOnce(),
        shipped: impl FnOnce(),
        cancelled: impl FnOnce(),
    ) {
        match self {
            OrderStatus::Draft => draft(),
            OrderStatus::Placed => placed(),
            OrderStatus::Shipped => shipped(),
            OrderStatus::Cancelled => cancelled(),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct OrderLine {
    product: ProductId,
    quantity: i64,
    unit_price: Money,
}

impl OrderLine {
    /// Creates a validated `OrderLine`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(product: ProductId, quantity: i64, unit_price: Money) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        Ok(Self {
            product,
            quantity,
            unit_price,
        })
    }

    pub fn product(&self) -> &ProductId { &self.product }

    pub fn quantity(&self) -> i64 { self.quantity }

    pub fn unit_price(&self) -> &Money { &self.unit_price }

    pub fn subtotal(&self) -> Money {
        self.unit_price.clone() * self.quantity
    }
}


/// A branded identity value.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct OrderId(String);

impl OrderId {
    pub fn new(value: impl Into<String>) -> Self { OrderId(value.into()) }
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Order {
    id: OrderId,
    customer: CustomerId,
    lines: Vec<OrderLine>,
    status: OrderStatus,
}

impl PartialEq for Order {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool { self.id == other.id }
}
impl Eq for Order {}
impl std::hash::Hash for Order {
    fn hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) { self.id.hash(state); }
}

impl Order {
    /// Creates a validated `Order`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(id: OrderId, customer: CustomerId, lines: Vec<OrderLine>, status: Option<OrderStatus>) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        let status = status.unwrap_or_else(|| OrderStatus::Draft);
        if lines.is_empty() && !(status == OrderStatus::Draft) {
            return Err(DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name: "Order", rule: "invariant failed" });
        }
        Ok(Self {
            id,
            customer,
            lines,
            status,
        })
    }

    pub fn id(&self) -> &OrderId { &self.id }

    pub fn customer(&self) -> &CustomerId { &self.customer }

    pub fn lines(&self) -> &Vec<OrderLine> { &self.lines }

    pub fn status(&self) -> OrderStatus { self.status }
}

/// Persistence-ignorant repository contract for the `Order` aggregate root.
pub trait OrderRepository {
    /// Loads the aggregate by its identity, if present.
    fn get(&self, id: &OrderId) -> Option<Order>;

    /// Persists a new aggregate.
    fn save(&mut self, aggregate: Order);

    /// Persists changes to an existing aggregate.
    fn update(&mut self, aggregate: Order);

    /// Removes the aggregate with the given identity.
    fn remove(&mut self, id: &OrderId);
}

/// A branded identity value.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ProductId(String);

impl ProductId {
    pub fn new(value: impl Into<String>) -> Self { ProductId(value.into()) }
    pub fn value(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
}

// ==== src/koine_runtime.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! Shared runtime support for the generated domain crate. Hand-edits will be overwritten.

#![allow(dead_code)]

/// Money-safe fixed-point decimal. Re-exported so generated modules can `use
/// crate::koine_runtime::Decimal;` without naming the backing crate.
pub use rust_decimal::Decimal;

/// A point in time. Aliased to the standard wall-clock type so the crate stays dependency-light.
pub type Instant = std::time::SystemTime;

/// The current instant (the Koine `now` value builtin).
pub fn now() -> Instant {
    std::time::SystemTime::now()
}

/// Parses a decimal literal at its exact textual form (money-safe, never via `f64`).
pub fn dec(text: &str) -> Decimal {
    Decimal::from_str_exact(text).expect("invalid decimal literal")
}

/// Narrows a Decimal to an i64 by truncating toward zero — the Koine `Int` result of a
/// scalar `*`/`/` that crossed through the Decimal representation. Panics if the truncated
/// value is out of i64 range (mirroring C#'s checked `(int)` decimal cast, which throws).
pub fn dec_to_i64(value: Decimal) -> i64 {
    use rust_decimal::prelude::ToPrimitive;
    value.trunc().to_i64().expect("decimal out of i64 range")
}

/// A closed interval `[start, end]` over an ordered type — the Koine `Range<T>`.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct Range<T: PartialOrd> {
    start: T,
    end: T,
}

impl<T: PartialOrd> Range<T> {
    /// Creates an interval. The caller is responsible for `start <= end` (a domain invariant).
    pub fn new(start: T, end: T) -> Self {
        Self { start, end }
    }

    pub fn start(&self) -> &T {
        &self.start
    }

    pub fn end(&self) -> &T {
        &self.end
    }

    /// True when `value` lies within the closed interval.
    pub fn contains(&self, value: &T) -> bool {
        *value >= self.start && *value <= self.end
    }

    /// True when this interval and `other` share at least one point.
    pub fn overlaps(&self, other: &Range<T>) -> bool {
        self.start <= other.end && other.start <= self.end
    }
}

/// Unanchored regex test (the Koine `matches /pattern/` operator). A malformed pattern — which
/// the compiler's regex validation rejects upstream — conservatively yields `false`.
pub fn regex_is_match(pattern: &str, input: &str) -> bool {
    regex::Regex::new(pattern).map(|re| re.is_match(input)).unwrap_or(false)
}

/// Decimal-safe sum over a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.sum(...)` fold). Panics on an empty
/// input, mirroring the seedless-fold behaviour of the C#/TypeScript/Python emitters.
pub fn koine_sum<T>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T
where
    T: std::ops::Add<Output = T>,
{
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| acc + x)
        .expect("sum of an empty collection")
}

/// The minimum of a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.min(...)` fold).
pub fn koine_min<T: PartialOrd>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T {
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| if x < acc { x } else { acc })
        .expect("min of an empty collection")
}

/// The maximum of a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.max(...)` fold).
pub fn koine_max<T: PartialOrd>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T {
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| if x > acc { x } else { acc })
        .expect("max of an empty collection")
}

// ==== src/lib.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! Generated domain crate. Hand-edits will be overwritten.

pub mod koine_runtime;
pub mod billing;
